programs.name: North Carolina Homestead Exemption
programs.what_it_does: This property tax relief program reduces the annual tax burden for qualifying seniors by exempting a portion of their home's assessed value from property taxes. It provides financial relief to help low-income elderly residents afford housing costs.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Residents aged 65 or older who meet county-specific income thresholds and own and occupy their primary residence.[1]
programs.whats_unique: State-specific property tax exemption tailored to NC seniors with income limits that vary by locality, beyond standard federal benefits.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Exemption up to $38,000-$45,000 of assessed value depending on county (varies yearly); can save hundreds to thousands in taxes annually.[1]

programs.name: North Carolina Seniors Farmers' Market Nutrition Program
programs.what_it_does: This program provides vouchers to eligible seniors to purchase fresh, unprepared fruits, vegetables, and herbs at farmers' markets and roadside stands. It promotes nutrition and supports local agriculture while improving access to healthy foods.[5]
programs.whats_unique: State-funded initiative specific to NC, matching federal SFMNP but administered with state resources for local markets.[5]
programs.benefit_value: $25-$50 in vouchers per season per participant.[5]

programs.name: Project C.A.R.E.
programs.what_it_does: Project C.A.R.E. (Community Assistance Reaching Everyone) offers financial assistance for non-medical home/community-based long-term care services to prevent or delay nursing home placement. It funds services like home care aides, adult day care, and home modifications through state and county funds.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors aged 65+ or adults 21+ who are frail, at risk of institutionalization, and meet income/asset limits.[5]
programs.whats_unique: State-county funded program unique to NC, providing cash payments for community care as an alternative to Medicaid long-term care.[5]
programs.benefit_value: Up to state maximum monthly (around $1,200-$3,500 depending on county and needs); lifetime cap applies.[1][5]

programs.name: NC Lifespan Respite Program
programs.what_it_does: This program provides respite services, training, and support to family caregivers of older adults and individuals with disabilities to prevent caregiver burnout. It funds short-term relief care, counseling, and education statewide.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of NC residents aged 60+ or adults with disabilities, particularly low-income households.[5]
programs.whats_unique: State-specific respite initiative under NC Division of Aging, expanding beyond federal caregiver support with targeted NC funding.[5]
programs.benefit_value: Vouchers or grants for respite services; amounts vary by need and availability (typically $10-$20/hour up to monthly limits).[5]
